sanskrit: महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा । मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः ॥
hindiTranslation: सात महर्षि और उनसे भी पूर्वमें होनेवाले चार सनकादि तथा चौदह मनु  ये सबकेसब मेरे मनसे पैदा हुए हैं और मेरेमें भाव श्रद्धाभक्ति रखनेवाले हैं, जिनकी संसारमें यह सम्पूर्ण प्रजा है ॥
englishTranslation: The seven great sages, the four ancient sages, and the fourteen Manus were born from my mind. The people of these worlds descend from them.
essence: All lineages and worlds emerge from Krishna’s mind.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says that the great sages, the ancient sages, and the Manus all arose from him, and all beings in these worlds descend through them.
simpleInsight: The world is not separate from the divine source that sustains it.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Krishna is answering Arjuna’s need to see beyond the battlefield. After naming his divine qualities, he points to the cosmic chain of creation: sages, primordial teachers, Manus, and all living beings. The teaching widens Arjuna’s vision from one war to the source of all life.
modernRelevance: You look at a family tree, a lineage of teachers, or the chain behind a tradition and wonder where it all began. This verse says the deepest source is not random history but a living divine origin.
translationLiteral: महर्षयः (great sages) / सप्त (seven) / पूर्वे (earlier) / चत्वारः (four) / मनवः (Manus) / तथा (and) / मद्भावाः (born from my being) / मानसा (from the mind) / जाताः (born) / येषाम् (from whom) / लोकाः (worlds) / इमाः (these) / प्रजाः (people)
insight: It shifts attention from isolated individuals to the sacred source behind all lineages and life.
